name: Charley's Slow Cooker Mexican Style Meat
instructions:
1. Prepare the chuck roast by trimming off any large pieces of excess fat from the surface, leaving a thin layer for flavor. Pat the meat dry with paper towels and season generously on all sides with salt and black pepper. 

2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it shimmers. The pan should be very hot for proper searing. 

3. Carefully place the seasoned roast in the hot skillet and sear for 3-4 minutes per side, turning to brown all sides evenly. You want a deep golden-brown crust. The meat doesn't need to be cooked through, just nicely browned on the outside. Transfer the browned roast to your slow cooker. 

4. In the same skillet (don't clean it - those browned bits add flavor), add the diced onion. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, for 3-4 minutes until the onion softens and becomes translucent. Add the onion to the slow cooker, scattering it over and around the roast. 

5. Add the green chile peppers, chili powder, cayenne pepper, hot sauce, and garlic powder directly to the slow cooker, distributing the seasonings evenly over the meat. 

6. Pour in enough water to come about 1/3 of the way up the sides of the roast. Don't submerge the meat - you want it to braise, not boil. 

7. Cover the slow cooker with the lid and set it to HIGH. Cook for 6 hours, checking occasionally to ensure there's still some liquid in the bottom. If it looks dry, add a splash more water. The meat should be getting very tender. 

8. After 6 hours, reduce the heat setting to LOW and continue cooking for another 2-4 hours, until the meat is fall-apart tender and shreds easily with a fork. The total cooking time will be 8-10 hours. You'll know it's done when you can easily pull the meat apart. 

9. Carefully transfer the roast to a large bowl or cutting board. Using two forks, shred the meat by pulling it apart into bite-sized pieces. Discard any large pieces of fat you encounter. 

10. If desired, reserve some of the cooking liquid from the slow cooker. You can skim off excess fat from the top if you like. This flavorful liquid can be used to moisten the shredded meat or as a sauce. 

11. Return the shredded meat to the slow cooker with some of the reserved liquid to keep it moist, or transfer to a serving bowl. 

12. Serve the tender, flavorful Mexican-style meat in warm tortillas for tacos, in large flour tortillas for burritos, over rice in burrito bowls, or however you prefer. Top with your favorite toppings like cheese, sour cream, salsa, guacamole, lettuce, and tomatoes.
simpleInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from roast and season with salt and pepper. 

2. Heat olive oil in skillet over medium-high heat. 

3. Sear roast 3-4 minutes per side until browned; transfer to slow cooker. 

4. Cook onion in same skillet 3-4 minutes; add to slow cooker. 

5. Add green chiles, chili powder, cayenne, hot sauce, and garlic powder to slow cooker. 

6. Add water to cover 1/3 of roast. 

7. Cover and cook on HIGH for 6 hours. 

8. Reduce to LOW and cook 2-4 hours more until meat shreds easily. 

9. Transfer meat to bowl and shred with two forks. 

10. Reserve 2 cups cooking liquid if desired. 

11. Return shredded meat to slow cooker with some liquid. 

12. Serve in tacos, burritos, or over rice with toppings.
